title: Boat Rental in Sardinia: Costs and Tips for 2026
html:
Exploring Sardinia by boat offers a unique perspective on its rugged coastline and crystal-clear waters. Renting a boat from Olbia or Cagliari typically costs between €200 and €3,000 per day, depending on the vessel and season. Whether you plan to hire a boat for a day or a week, the 2026 market provides plenty of verified options through BoatHire24.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Sardinia?

Prices range from €200 for a shared excursion to over €3,000 for a crewed luxury yacht per day. Costs vary widely, so understanding the options is key. A small motorboat for half a day without a skipper might cost €350, while a full-day catamaran charter for up to 10 people averages €1,200. For a premium experience, a crewed 17-metre yacht such as the Azimut 58 for hire, available on BoatHire24, can exceed €2,500 daily. Always factor in fuel, mooring fees, and any skipper charges, which are often extra. You can hire a boat in a few minutes.

To compare verified boat listings and find the best value, you can hire a boat through BoatHire24's Sardinia page.

Where to set off in Sardinia

The main departure points are scattered along the coast, each with its own character. Starting from Olbia's Porto San Paolo gives quick access to the La Maddalena Archipelago, a popular cruising ground. In the north, the glamorous Porto Cervo marina in the Costa Smeralda offers luxury facilities. On the west coast, Alghero's port is a gateway to the Neptune's Grotto sea caves. South of Cagliari, the Poetto beach waterfront provides a laid-back base. For a more remote feel, the small harbour of Cala Gonone on the east coast leads to stunning coves like Cala Luna. Each marina has its own charm, and distances between them can be a day's sail. One option worth a look is the Azimut 58 for hire.

Which boat to rent

Choosing the right boat depends on your group size and itinerary. Here are four popular options available through BoatHire24.

  1. Rigid inflatable boat (RIB), best for exploring hidden coves and short trips; up to 6 guests; from €300 half-day.
  2. Small motorboat (up to 7 metres), ideal for couples or small families; up to 5 guests; from €400 half-day.
  3. Sailing catamaran (12-14 metres), spacious and stable for longer cruises; up to 10 guests; from €1,000 full-day.
  4. Crewed motor yacht (15-20 metres), luxury with a skipper and host; up to 12 guests; from €2,500 full-day.

Cost breakdown by boat type

A shared group tour with a local skipper costs around €80 per person for a half-day, including snorkelling equipment. Renting a small motorboat without a skipper for a half-day typically runs €250 to €450. A modern catamaran for a full-day charter, such as a Lagoon 42, costs between €1,000 and €1,500 in low season. For a fully crewed yacht with a dedicated skipper, such as the Azimut 58 mentioned earlier, prices start at €2,000 and can exceed €3,000 per day during peak July and August. Most rentals require a refundable deposit of €500 to €2,000, depending on boat value. If you own a boat, you can rent out your boat and earn from spare days.

To understand the booking process and what to expect, read how boat rental works on BoatHire24's resource page.

Seasons and licence requirements

The high season runs from July to August, when the scirocco wind can affect the northern coasts. Spring and autumn offer milder weather and lower prices. If you wish to rent a bareboat (without a skipper), you typically need a recognised boating licence, such as the ICC or a national equivalent. The operator will confirm exact requirements before booking. For skippered charters, no licence is needed, as the skipper handles navigation. To see how booking works, visit how boat rental works.

A local tip: anchor early in Cala Mariolu, east of Cala Gonone, to secure a spot in the turquoise cove before the crowds arrive from midday.

At a glance

  • Price band: €80 to €3,000 per day
  • Peak months: July and August
  • Typical trip length: half-day or full-day
  • Maximum guests: up to 12 on most yachts
  • Currency: EUR

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Sardinia?

Yes, a licence is required for bareboat rentals without a skipper, but not for skippered charters. Most Italian authorities recognise the International Certificate of Competence (ICC) for pleasure craft. For smaller motorboats under 10 metres, a simple recreational licence may suffice. Always confirm with the boat listing on BoatHire24, as requirements can vary. No licence is needed if you hire a skipper, which is the most common choice for visitors.

Questions about boat rental in Sardinia

Can I rent a boat for a single day in Sardinia?

Yes, many operators offer half-day or full-day rentals, especially for small motorboats and RIBs, with flexible booking.

What is the best time of year for boat rental in Sardinia?

May, June, and September offer warm weather, fewer crowds, and competitive prices, while July and August are busiest and most expensive.

Are there hidden fees like fuel or cleaning?

Fuel, mooring fees, and a final cleaning charge are often not included in the base price; check the listing details carefully before booking.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Sardinia or list your own. For anyone considering renting out their boat, the platform makes it easy to reach a global audience and manage bookings securely.

If you own a boat in Sardinia, you can list on BoatHire24 and earn income by renting it to verified guests. The sign-up process is straightforward, and you set your own availability and pricing. Visit the owner page to rent out your boat.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Sardinia and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
